meaning: adj.爱出风头的,漂亮,时髦的,炫耀的n.<苏格兰>活跃的帅小伙子
examples: We all colluded in the myth of him as the swanky businessman.
He would make sure we had seats and invitations to the swanky presos too.
When you move to a new place, don't immediately go for the swanky new apartment.
The two allegedly met at a swanky Washington hotel on the night before Valentine's Day.
Birmingham's once-barren Gas Street Basin has already been transformed into a swanky new development.
